title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jules and Gwendolyn discuss a new theory regarding the disappearance of Helen Hardy. Jules proposes the existence of an unknown factor, "X," representing a person not yet identified. He hypothesizes that Helen was preparing to run away with a lover and that Kelvin, discovering her note, strangled her in a fit of rage. Jules suggests Helen may not have died but instead regained consciousness and fled, which would explain why Kelvin believed he had killed her and why her belongings were missing. Gwendolyn remains skeptical, insisting she saw a dead body during her childhood.

Their discussion is interrupted by Miss Marple. After social pleasantries, Gwendolyn discloses their findings and asks if her father might have been involved. Miss Marple suggests it is possible, noting that such strangulation cases often involve husbands, and agrees that an unknown element may be at play. Gwendolyn then announces her intention to visit the Salt Marsh Sanatorium in Norfolk, where her father died, to search for clues.

At the sanatorium, Jules and Gwendolyn meet Dr. Penrose. Gwendolyn explains that she was raised in New Zealand and has only recently learned the details of her father's past. Dr. Penrose reviews Colonel Hardy's medical records, noting that the Colonel suffered from intense delusions or hallucinations. He reveals that the Colonel was extremely agitated and insisted he had strangled his second wife out of jealousy. Dr. Penrose admits that without Dr. Kennedy's prior assurance that the wife was still alive, he would have believed the Colonel's claims of murder.
